stakeholders.name: Ahmad Abdullayev
stakeholders.authority: final decision authority for the foundation selections

selected_project_governance_baseline.purpose: internal engineering and governance baseline only

legal_sources.authority: President of the Republic of Azerbaijan official website
legal_sources.title: Constitution of the Republic of Azerbaijan
legal_sources.project_proposition: Intellectual property is protected; private-life information and personal information receive constitutional protection, including correction or elimination of inaccurate or unlawfully collected information.

proposition_mappings.proposition: Intellectual property is protected.
Private life and personal information are protected, with correction or elimination remedies for inaccurate or unlawfully collected information.

legal_sources.currency_note: Official constitutional text published by the President of the Republic of Azerbaijan.

legal_sources.authority: Unified Electronic Database of Legal Acts of the Republic of Azerbaijan
legal_sources.title: Law of the Republic of Azerbaijan on Personal Data, No. 998-IIIQ
legal_sources.project_proposition: Before production personal-data processing, the project must determine legal applicability, purpose and basis, the Article 8.6 consent channel, licensing, collection disclosures, rights channels and deadlines, security and audit controls, Article 14 transfer findings, and registration or a documented clause-specific exemption.

proposition_mappings.proposition: The household or personal-use exception is fact-specific and is not assumed for this public professional website.
The collection notice and rights workflow must implement statutory fields, request channels, and working-day deadlines.
EHIS consent-channel and licensing applicability require explicit determinations before processing or operation.
Cross-border findings and registration or clause-specific exemption evidence are release gates.

legal_sources.currency_note: Current consolidated official e-qanun text reviewed, including Article 8.6.

legal_sources.authority: Unified Electronic Database of Legal Acts of the Republic of Azerbaijan
legal_sources.title: Cabinet of Ministers Decision No. 237 on personal-data information systems not requiring state registration
legal_sources.project_proposition: Registration exemptions are purpose- and fact-specific; the project records an exemption only with evidence matching the applicable clause and otherwise follows the registration path.

proposition_mappings.proposition: An exemption requires a documented match to the applicable purpose, subject category, subject-count and other clause conditions; project size alone is insufficient.

legal_sources.currency_note: Current consolidated official text, replacing the stale Ministry PDF and including the 29 December 2024 amendment to clause 2.3.

legal_sources.authority: Ministry of Digital Development and Transport of the Republic of Azerbaijan
legal_sources.title: Cabinet of Ministers Decision No. 149 on state registration and cancellation of personal-data information systems
legal_sources.project_proposition: The registration evidence package must identify the system owner, processing basis and purposes, data and subject categories, safeguards, users, transfers, audit, and rights procedures; the current Ministry name is supplied by the tracked 2022 amendment.

proposition_mappings.proposition: The project must prepare the registration application, evidence package, review inputs and change/cancellation controls before production collection.

legal_sources.currency_note: Official Ministry PDF must be read with LAW-AZ-CABINET-149-161-2022-AMENDMENT because its authority-name amendment list is not current.

legal_sources.authority: Unified Electronic Database of Legal Acts of the Republic of Azerbaijan
legal_sources.title: Cabinet of Ministers Decision No. 137 of 8 April 2022 amending Decisions No. 149 and No. 161
legal_sources.project_proposition: References in Decisions No. 149 and No. 161 to the predecessor ministry are read as references to the Ministry of Digital Development and Transport.

proposition_mappings.proposition: The current competent-ministry name is used in registration and security governance records.

legal_sources.currency_note: Official amendment text affecting both Decision No. 149 and Decision No. 161.

legal_sources.authority: Ministry of Digital Development and Transport of the Republic of Azerbaijan
legal_sources.title: Cabinet of Ministers Decision No. 161 approving Requirements for the Protection of Personal Data
provisions: Operative protection requirements, read with current amendments
legal_sources.project_proposition: Production requires a clause-mapped security evidence package covering threats and protection level, approved software and instructions, restoration, system and protection documentation, access and audit registers, physical controls, authentication and authorization, encryption, certification and state expertise as applicable.

proposition_mappings.proposition: A generic security assertion is insufficient; each applicable Decision No. 161 control requires implementation evidence and amendment-aware review.

legal_sources.currency_note: Official Ministry text; repealed or amended clauses must be excluded through a current clause-level review, including the 2022 authority-name amendment.

legal_sources.authority: President of the Republic of Azerbaijan official website
legal_sources.title: Regulation on the Electronic Government Information System, including the Digital Consent subsystem
legal_sources.project_proposition: If Article 8.6 applies, the consent and withdrawal design must use the legally required EHIS or Digital Consent channel; an ordinary privacy acknowledgment is not substituted for that determination.

proposition_mappings.proposition: EHIS/Digital Consent integration and evidence requirements are resolved before consent-dependent collection is activated.

legal_sources.currency_note: Current official Presidential publication reviewed together with Personal Data Law Article 8.6.

legal_sources.authority: Ministry of Digital Development and Transport of the Republic of Azerbaijan
legal_sources.title: Licensing information for formation of personal-data resources and creation or servicing of information systems
provisions: Personal-data system licensing service and application requirements
legal_sources.project_proposition: The project must determine and evidence whether its formation, creation or servicing activities require a special permission or licence; registration does not answer that separate question.

proposition_mappings.proposition: No personal-data information system is operated until Article 9.14 licensing applicability and any required evidence are resolved.

legal_sources.currency_note: Current Ministry service page; substantive applicability remains a recorded legal determination rather than an assumption from the page alone.

legal_sources.authority: Ministry of Digital Development and Transport of the Republic of Azerbaijan
legal_sources.title: Current official personal-data information-system registry page
legal_sources.project_proposition: The current competent-authority registry and registration route must be checked and archived before any registration-dependent production activation.

proposition_mappings.proposition: Registration evidence refers to the current Ministry registry rather than only a historical procedure PDF.

legal_sources.currency_note: Mutable official authority page; re-retrieve and archive it at the release review.

legal_sources.authority: Unified Electronic Database of Legal Acts of the Republic of Azerbaijan
legal_sources.title: Law of the Republic of Azerbaijan on Private International Law
legal_sources.project_proposition: Azerbaijan is a provisional internal governance baseline; applicable law, contractual choice-of-law effect, mandatory rules and any court forum require a separate nexus and enforceability determination.

proposition_mappings.proposition: The project does not convert an internal baseline or unilateral informational notice into a conclusive applicable-law or forum determination.

legal_sources.currency_note: Current consolidated official e-qanun text reviewed for the limited project proposition stated here.

legal_sources.authority: Unified Electronic Database of Legal Acts of the Republic of Azerbaijan
legal_sources.title: Law of the Republic of Azerbaijan on Copyright and Related Rights, No. 115-IQ
legal_sources.project_proposition: Publication requires a recorded rights basis, licence and attribution where applicable; possession of an asset is not treated as ownership of copyright.

proposition_mappings.proposition: Copyright protects expression rather than ideas or methods, and ownership or possession of a copy does not itself transfer copyright.
Publication and enforcement controls require item-level authorship, licence and attribution evidence.

legal_sources.title: Regulation (EU) 2016/679
legal_sources.project_proposition: GDPR applicability to the owner or controller is contingent on the Article 3 establishment, offering-of-goods-or-services, or monitoring tests; choosing a German AWS region alone does not establish that applicability and does not itself satisfy Azerbaijan Article 14.

proposition_mappings.proposition: The GDPR territorial-scope determination remains pending; German hosting alone is not recorded as a trigger.
If the GDPR applies, its substantive controller and processor duties enter the release control set.

legal_sources.currency_note: Official consolidated EU regulation; applicability remains contingent pending the Article 3 facts.

legal_sources.title: Guidelines 3/2018 on the territorial scope of the GDPR (Article 3), Version 2.1
provisions: Article 3 establishment, targeting and monitoring guidance
legal_sources.project_proposition: The owner/controller territorial-scope analysis must use establishment, targeting and monitoring facts and may not infer GDPR scope solely from infrastructure location.

proposition_mappings.proposition: A documented factual territorial-scope analysis is a production gate if EU-facing processing is contemplated.

legal_sources.currency_note: Final adopted EDPB guidance supporting interpretation of GDPR Article 3.

legal_sources.title: AWS Regions and Availability Zones
legal_sources.project_proposition: AWS identifies eu-central-1 as Europe (Frankfurt), Germany, with three Availability Zones; this establishes region identity, not end-to-end data residency.

proposition_mappings.proposition: Regional resources selected by the project must use eu-central-1 and separately prove each service's actual processing paths.

legal_sources.title: AWS Data Privacy FAQ
legal_sources.project_proposition: AWS states that customers select the region for customer content, while account information, resource identifiers, service behavior and legal demands require separate treatment; the project therefore records service-specific evidence instead of asserting an absolute region lock.

proposition_mappings.proposition: Provider documentation, applicable terms and actual service configuration must be archived for each processing path.

legal_sources.currency_note: Mutable official provider page; re-retrieve it and the applicable contract terms at release review.

legal_sources.title: Amazon SES regions and regional resource behavior
provisions: Region-specific endpoints, identities, credentials, suppression and feedback settings
legal_sources.project_proposition: SES identities, credentials and operational settings are region-specific, but outbound delivery reaches recipient infrastructure and must not be described as region-confined.

proposition_mappings.proposition: Use eu-central-1 settings and inventory delivery events, feedback paths and recipient systems separately.

legal_sources.currency_note: Current official Amazon SES regional documentation.

legal_sources.title: Data protection in Amazon Simple Email Service
provisions: Sensitive data in tags and names
Encryption in transit and opportunistic TLS
legal_sources.project_proposition: Personal data is prohibited in SES tags or resource-name fields, engagement tracking is disabled, and delivery must require TLS or carry only a minimal notification whose substantive content remains in a secured administrative system.

proposition_mappings.proposition: The release evidence must prove metadata minimization and the selected delivery-TLS or minimal-notification control.

legal_sources.currency_note: Current official Amazon SES data-protection documentation; SES uses opportunistic TLS by default unless stricter behavior is configured.

role_assignments.accountability: prepare canonical drafts, evidence and provenance
approve factual meaning, currency and public claims
approve personal-data, rights, consent, retention and policy effects
publish only the approved immutable revision and preserve rollback

rules: one canonical record per authoritative fact
no public claim without an owner-approved source and provenance
no third-party asset without recorded rights, licence and attribution
no third-party personal data without a recorded lawful publication basis
draft, private, restricted, embargoed, revoked and unapproved values never enter public derivatives
approval and publication are separately timestamped decisions even when one person fills both roles
missing evidence results in omission or an explicit draft state, never invented content
material correction preserves the previous revision and publishes a correction record when public trust requires it

review_schedules.name: current and release-sensitive
review_schedules.applies_to: contact routes, current opportunities, temporary launch copy, active calls to action and incident-sensitive notices
review_schedules.overdue_action: remove or unpublish time-sensitive claims until reviewed

review_schedules.name: active academic, identity, legal and policy
review_schedules.applies_to: identity, current roles, research state, publications, CV, privacy and legal controls
review_schedules.overdue_action: block new release and mark the record for owner review

review_schedules.name: assets, licences and stable supporting material
review_schedules.applies_to: media, documents, licences, attribution, localized variants and stable supporting copy
review_schedules.overdue_action: block replacement publication and review continued public availability

review_schedules.name: historical and archival
review_schedules.applies_to: completed education, historical roles, archived activities and other stable dated records
review_schedules.overdue_action: queue content audit before the next release

event_review_triggers.trigger: owner or subject reports an inaccuracy
event_review_triggers.response: contain misleading publication immediately and complete triage within two business days

event_review_triggers.trigger: role, affiliation, contact route, availability or opportunity changes
event_review_triggers.response: review every dependent page and derivative before or at the effective time

event_review_triggers.trigger: publication correction, withdrawal, retraction or identifier conflict
event_review_triggers.response: freeze automatic propagation and complete owner review before republication

event_review_triggers.trigger: rights, privacy, consent-withdrawal, impersonation or takedown concern
event_review_triggers.response: acknowledge within two business days, restrict when risk warrants, preserve evidence and record the decision

event_review_triggers.trigger: law, jurisdiction, provider, region, subprocessor, purpose or data-field change
event_review_triggers.response: complete legal-privacy impact review before the changed processing activates

event_review_triggers.trigger: security incident or unauthorized public exposure
event_review_triggers.response: contain immediately, preserve an audit record and initiate the incident workflow

event_review_triggers.trigger: canonical route, external destination or downloadable asset becomes unavailable
event_review_triggers.response: restore, redirect or remove the broken claim without losing canonical history

content_inventory.label: Canonical owner name
content_inventory.publication_gate: canonical value equals Ahmad Abdullayev in every public and machine-readable representation

content_inventory.label: Temporary verified-details notice
content_inventory.publication_gate: remove when approved biography and positioning content launch

content_inventory.label: Temporary public primary-audience list
content_inventory.publication_gate: remove from the public homepage before content-complete launch

content_inventory.label: Temporary public visitor-goal list
content_inventory.publication_gate: remove from the public homepage before content-complete launch

content_inventory.label: Generic foundation metadata description and footer copy
content_inventory.publication_gate: replace with accurate owner-approved positioning and policy navigation before launch

content_inventory.label: Preferred name forms and public disambiguation
content_inventory.publication_gate: owner-approved non-sensitive disambiguation evidence exists

content_inventory.label: Current and historical titles, roles and affiliations
content_inventory.publication_gate: each role has owner-approved organization, title, dates, state and evidence

content_inventory.label: Short professional biography

content_inventory.label: Medium, extended and media biography variants
content_inventory.publication_gate: variants derive from one approved fact set and contain no contradictions

content_inventory.label: Portrait, alternative text and optional pronunciation assets
content_inventory.publication_gate: consent, copyright, licence, attribution and accessibility treatment are recorded

content_inventory.label: Structured expertise areas and evidence
content_inventory.publication_gate: every material expertise claim has an approved evidence relationship

content_inventory.label: Research overview, areas, questions and methods
content_inventory.publication_gate: owner-approved specialist and public explanations exist

content_inventory.label: Research activities, collaborators, institutions and funding
content_inventory.publication_gate: dates, contribution, third-party publication basis and funding terms are approved

content_inventory.label: Publication core metadata and identifiers
content_inventory.publication_gate: title, venue, status, dates and identifiers are owner-approved and deduplicated

content_inventory.label: Publication authors, contributions, versions and resources
content_inventory.publication_gate: author order, contribution, version status, resource rights and correction state are verified

content_inventory.label: Project records, memberships, outcomes and evidence
content_inventory.publication_gate: owner contribution, confidentiality, team attribution and evidence are approved

content_inventory.label: Software and dataset outputs
content_inventory.publication_gate: licence, release, repository, data access, security and ethical limitations are approved

content_inventory.label: Education and thesis records
content_inventory.publication_gate: institution, program, degree state, dates, thesis and supervisor publication basis are approved

content_inventory.label: Experience and appointment records
content_inventory.publication_gate: position, organization, dates, current state and outcomes are owner-approved

content_inventory.label: Teaching, courses and mentoring
content_inventory.publication_gate: restricted materials and student or mentee identities are excluded unless publication is approved

content_inventory.label: Awards, grants, talks and service
content_inventory.publication_gate: title, organization, role, date, state and evidence are approved

content_inventory.label: News, articles and media appearances
content_inventory.publication_gate: authorship, source, dates, external rights, embeds and correction state are approved

content_inventory.label: Current CV and accessible equivalent
content_inventory.publication_gate: approved current version contains no private data and has an accessible equivalent

content_inventory.label: Downloadable documents, images, audio, video and supplements
content_inventory.publication_gate: checksum, malware scan, rights, attribution, privacy and accessibility metadata pass

content_inventory.label: Contact form, collaboration availability and response expectations
content_inventory.publication_gate: registration or exemption evidence, transfer assessment, provider terms, notice, consent, retention and delivery tests pass

content_inventory.label: Privacy and storage notice
content_inventory.publication_gate: notice exactly matches enabled fields, purposes, recipients, region, retention, rights and contact route

content_inventory.label: Rights, licence and attribution notice
content_inventory.publication_gate: notice does not claim ownership or grant a licence beyond item-level evidence

content_inventory.label: Rights, privacy, impersonation, inaccuracy and takedown route
content_inventory.publication_gate: reporting route, evidence protection, acknowledgement, triage, containment, decision and appeal path are tested

content_inventory.label: Canonical routes, redirects and navigation labels
content_inventory.publication_gate: every active route is unique, canonical, authorized for public visibility and loop-free

content_inventory.label: Structured metadata, Atom feed and static social-preview content
content_inventory.publication_gate: derive only from the exact approved public snapshot and approved assets

content_inventory.label: English source-locale content and language metadata
content_inventory.publication_gate: every public route and metadata representation declares English consistently

content_inventory.label: Legal source register, governance baseline, revisions and approvals
content_inventory.publication_gate: machine validation passes and every decision remains append-only and attributable

steps.name: Freeze and identify sources
steps.required_inputs: read-only source export, source owner, export time, format, counts and checksums
steps.exit_criteria: every source has an immutable manifest entry and no source is silently overwritten
steps.rollback: discard the working copy and recreate it from the immutable source export

steps.name: Discover and enumerate
steps.required_inputs: source manifest and the 32-item content inventory
steps.exit_criteria: every discovered record and asset maps to one inventory key or a documented exclusion
steps.rollback: restore the prior inventory revision and rerun discovery

steps.name: Classify rights, privacy and visibility
steps.required_inputs: discovered records, rights evidence, consent and confidentiality obligations
steps.exit_criteria: each item has rights status, privacy class, intended visibility and disposition; unknowns are held unpublished
steps.rollback: return affected items to quarantine and remove them from all candidate public sets

steps.name: Normalize and deduplicate canonical facts
steps.required_inputs: classified records, identifiers, dates and relationship candidates
steps.exit_criteria: canonical keys are unique, identifiers are normalized, conflicts are explicit and no display copy becomes a second authority
steps.rollback: discard normalized candidates and retain the untouched source manifest

steps.name: Transform into versioned drafts
steps.required_inputs: approved mapping specification and normalized candidates
steps.exit_criteria: schema-valid draft revisions preserve source identifiers, provenance and source-to-target field mappings
steps.rollback: delete unapproved draft imports by migration batch identifier

steps.name: Verify facts and relationships
steps.required_inputs: draft revisions, evidence and conflict report
steps.exit_criteria: material claims, dates, states, authorship, relationships and identifiers are approved or held unpublished
steps.rollback: reject the affected draft revision without changing the public snapshot

steps.name: Validate assets and accessibility
steps.required_inputs: quarantined assets, file metadata, captions, transcripts and document equivalents
steps.exit_criteria: checksums, type and malware checks, alternatives, captions, transcripts and accessible-document checks pass
steps.rollback: keep failed assets quarantined and remove dependent public actions

steps.name: Complete legal, privacy and rights review
steps.required_inputs: candidate public set, processing inventory, rights ledger and policy drafts
steps.exit_criteria: registration or exemption evidence, transfer controls, notices, retention, licences, attribution and takedown route pass review
steps.rollback: hold the affected content or processing feature inactive

steps.name: Approve immutable release candidate
steps.required_inputs: content, accessibility, security, privacy and operations evidence for one immutable candidate
steps.exit_criteria: separate approval decisions identify the same revision and artifact checksums
steps.rollback: reject the candidate and preserve the last approved release

steps.name: Publish atomically
steps.required_inputs: approved release candidate and verified rollback target
steps.exit_criteria: one transaction or equivalent atomic workflow activates the approved snapshot without exposing drafts
steps.rollback: reactivate the last verified public snapshot

steps.name: Rebuild derivatives and reconcile
steps.required_inputs: published revision identifier and source manifest
steps.exit_criteria: search, routes, sitemap, feed, metadata, social previews, caches and public assets use the same snapshot and reconciliation has zero unexplained variance
steps.rollback: invalidate failed derivatives and serve the last coherent derivative set

steps.name: Archive evidence and close migration
steps.required_inputs: manifest, mappings, validation output, approvals, release identifier and reconciliation report
steps.exit_criteria: the evidence pack is immutable, restoration is demonstrated and every source item has a final disposition
steps.rollback: reopen the migration batch through a new revision; never rewrite the closed evidence pack

revision_history.summary: Recorded the initial repository, product baseline, architecture, development contract and executable foundation checks in Git commit 62418c3.

impacts.data_migration: established the initial repository state; no legacy academic content was migrated
impacts.security_privacy: established public/private boundaries and prohibited unsupported public attributes
impacts.accessibility: established WCAG 2.2 AA as the conformance target

revision_history.actor_role: implementation agent acting under owner instruction
revision_history.summary: Established the complete decision, governance, evidence and traceability bundle for foundation items 11 through 23.

impacts.data_migration: creates an approved plan and dispositions; performs no production content mutation
impacts.security_privacy: adds mandatory registration, cross-border, minimization, rights, provider and publication controls
impacts.accessibility: requires accessibility evidence before asset or document migration approval
impacts.testing: adds schema, semantic, cross-artifact, source-regeneration and immutable-bundle validation

approval_records.decision: Approve the selected scope, jurisdiction, content-governance, inventory, migration, revision and traceability foundations as the project baseline.
approval_records.scope_limit: foundation-scope decision only; it is not feature-release, production-deployment or whole-SRS conformance approval

approval_records.decision: Approve the canonical ownership, source-evidence rule, four review cadences, seven event triggers, 32-item inventory and explicit unpublished dispositions for unsupplied content.
approval_records.scope_limit: foundation-scope decision only; each future public content revision requires its own evidence and approval

approval_records.decision: Approve the foundation requirement that migrated documents, media, language metadata and complete workflows carry accessibility evidence before public approval.
approval_records.scope_limit: foundation-scope decision only; no page, document or complete process is represented as accessibility-conformant by this record

approval_records.decision: Approve the foundation public/private boundary, quarantined migration, minimized processing, immutable evidence and provider-control gates.
approval_records.scope_limit: foundation-scope decision only; it is not a production threat-model, penetration-test or security-release approval

approval_records.decision: Approve Azerbaijan as the provisional internal governance baseline pending actual nexus and applicable-law determination, eu-central-1 as the selected but not yet deployed region, and the registration, licensing, consent-channel, certification, cross-border, notice, rights, retention and inactive-feature gates.
approval_records.scope_limit: foundation-scope decision only; it does not waive mandatory law or approve production personal-data collection

approval_records.decision: Approve the 12-step migration, reconciliation, atomic-publication, rollback, evidence-archive and append-only revision controls for foundation use.
approval_records.scope_limit: foundation-scope decision only; it is not deployment, restoration-drill or production-release approval
